rationale: VDA ISA 1.1.1 requires a documented IS policy approved by top management; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.1 identically requires policies for information security approved by management and communicated to relevant parties.

VDA ISA 1.1.1 policy approval requirement is a subset of ISO 27001 clause 5.2 (Leadership and commitment) which additionally requires strategic direction and resource allocation beyond policy existence.
VDA ISA 1.1.2 requires regular review and update of the IS policy; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.1 explicitly requires policies to be reviewed at defined intervals or when significant changes occur.
VDA ISA 1.1.2 policy review is a subset of ISO 27001 clause 10.2 (Continual improvement) which covers the broader PDCA cycle beyond individual policy documents.
VDA ISA 1.2.1 requires defined and assigned IS roles and responsibilities; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.2 identically requires information security roles and responsibilities to be defined and allocated.
VDA ISA 1.2.2 requires an appointed Information Security Officer (ISB/CISO); ISO 27001:2022 A.5.2 requires assignment of responsibilities including a designated IS management function.
VDA ISA 1.2.2 ISO mandate requirement maps to ISO 27001 clause 5.3 which requires top management to assign and communicate IS roles and responsibilities within the organisation.
VDA ISA 1.2.3 requires segregation of duties for IS-relevant functions; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.3 identically requires conflicting duties and areas of responsibility to be segregated.
VDA ISA 1.2.4 requires management to actively support and demonstrate commitment to IS; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.4 requires management to direct and support persons contributing to IS effectiveness.
VDA ISA 1.2.5 requires maintaining contact with relevant authorities (e.g. regulators, law enforcement); ISO 27001:2022 A.5.5 identically requires maintaining contacts with relevant authorities.
VDA ISA 1.2.6 requires membership or contact with special interest groups (CERTs, industry groups); ISO 27001:2022 A.5.6 requires contacts with special interest groups and professional associations.
VDA ISA 1.3.1 requires an inventory of information assets and associated systems; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.9 requires an inventory of information and other associated assets to be maintained.
VDA ISA 1.3.1 asset inventory partially overlaps with ISO 27001:2022 A.5.12 (classification) but stops short of requiring full classification levels — only identification and ownership.

VDA ISA 1.3.2 requires classification of information based on protection needs (confidentiality levels); ISO 27001:2022 A.5.12 identically requires classification of information based on confidentiality, integrity and availability needs.
VDA ISA 1.3.2 requires appropriate labelling of classified information assets; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.13 identically requires labelling of information in accordance with the classification scheme.
VDA ISA 1.3.3 requires rules for acceptable use of information assets (handling rules per class); ISO 27001:2022 A.5.10 requires policies for acceptable use of information and associated assets.

VDA ISA 1.3.4 (new in v6) requires a software approval process before installation on operational systems; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.19 identically requires controls governing installation of software on operational systems.
VDA ISA 1.3.4 software approval process relates to configuration management (A.8.9) but is narrower, covering authorisation for new software rather than full configuration baseline management.

VDA ISA 1.4.1 requires a risk management process to identify and treat IS risks; ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.1.2 requires an information security risk assessment process including risk identification and criteria.
VDA ISA 1.4.1 risk treatment requirement maps directly to ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.1.3 which requires selection and documentation of risk treatment options and controls.
VDA ISA 1.4.2 requires compliance verification through internal audits; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.36 requires compliance with policies, rules and standards for IS to be regularly reviewed.
VDA ISA 1.4.2 internal audit requirement is co-extensive with ISO 27001 clause 9.2 (Internal audit) which requires planned audit programmes to assess ISMS conformity.
VDA ISA 1.4.3 requires management review of IS at defined intervals; ISO 27001:2022 clause 9.3 identically requires top management to review the ISMS at planned intervals.

VDA ISA 1.4.4 requires independent review of information security (external or internal); ISO 27001:2022 A.5.35 identically requires independent review of IS at planned intervals or when significant changes occur.
VDA ISA 1.5.1 requires rules for transferring information with third parties including NDA; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.14 requires policies and procedures for information transfer to be in place.
VDA ISA 1.5.2 requires IS requirements to be included in supplier relationships; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.19 identically requires information security in supplier relationships to be managed.
VDA ISA 1.5.2 contractual IS requirements with suppliers map to ISO 27001:2022 A.5.20 which requires addressing IS within supplier agreements.
VDA ISA 1.5.3 requires monitoring and review of supplier services; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.22 identically requires monitoring, review and change management of supplier services.
VDA ISA 1.5.4 requires managing IS risks in the ICT supply chain; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.21 identically requires managing information security in the ICT supply chain.
VDA ISA 1.6.1 (revised) requires a documented incident reporting and response process; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.24 identically requires IS incident management planning and preparation.
VDA ISA 1.6.1 reporting of security incidents by personnel maps to ISO 27001:2022 A.6.8 which requires mechanisms for personnel to report IS events.
VDA ISA 1.6.2 (new in v6) management of security incidents including assessment/triage maps to ISO 27001:2022 A.5.25 (assessment and decision on information security events).
VDA ISA 1.6.2 management of security incidents including response and remediation maps to ISO 27001:2022 A.5.26 (response to information security incidents).
VDA ISA 1.6.2 requires post-incident lessons-learned analysis; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.27 identically requires learning from information security incidents.

VDA ISA 1.6.2 incident management partially covers evidence collection but ISO 27001:2022 A.5.28 (collection of evidence) is broader, covering forensic and legal evidentiary requirements not fully spelled out in ISA.

VDA ISA 1.6.3 (new in v6) dealing with crisis/emergency situations maps to ISO 27001:2022 A.5.29 (information security during disruption) requiring continuity of IS controls during adverse conditions.
VDA ISA 1.6.3 crisis management and IT service continuity requirements map to ISO 27001:2022 A.5.30 (ICT readiness for business continuity) requiring ICT continuity plans tested and maintained.
VDA ISA 2.1.1 requires background screening/vetting of personnel before and during employment; ISO 27001:2022 A.6.1 identically requires screening of all candidates for employment.
VDA ISA 2.1.2 requires IS obligations to be reflected in employment terms; ISO 27001:2022 A.6.2 identically requires IS responsibilities stated in terms and conditions of employment.
VDA ISA 2.1.3 requires confidentiality/NDA agreements for all personnel with access to sensitive information; ISO 27001:2022 A.6.6 identically requires NDAs or confidentiality agreements.
VDA ISA 2.2.1 requires IS awareness training for all employees; ISO 27001:2022 A.6.3 identically requires IS awareness, education and training appropriate to job function.
VDA ISA 2.2.1 training requirement is a subset of ISO 27001 clause 7.2 (Competence) which additionally requires determining necessary competence and retaining evidence of competence.
VDA ISA 2.2.2 requires role-specific IS training for personnel with elevated IS responsibilities; ISO 27001:2022 A.6.3 covers education and training appropriate to the person's job function.

VDA ISA 2.3.1 requires revoking access rights and recovering assets when employment ends or changes; ISO 27001:2022 A.6.5 identically requires responsibilities after termination or change of employment.
VDA ISA 2.3.1 asset return upon termination maps to ISO 27001:2022 A.5.11 which requires return of all organisational assets by employees upon termination.
VDA ISA 2.3.2 requires a formal disciplinary process for IS policy violations; ISO 27001:2022 A.6.4 identically requires a disciplinary process for personnel who have committed an IS policy breach.
VDA ISA 3.1.1 requires defined physical security perimeters protecting sensitive areas; ISO 27001:2022 A.7.1 identically requires physical security perimeters to be defined and used.
VDA ISA 3.1.1 securing offices, server rooms, and sensitive facilities maps to ISO 27001:2022 A.7.3 (securing offices, rooms and facilities).

VDA ISA 3.1.2 requires physical entry controls (keycards, locks, airlocks) to restrict access to secure areas; ISO 27001:2022 A.7.2 identically requires physical entry controls protecting secure areas.

VDA ISA 3.1.3 requires physical security monitoring including CCTV and intrusion detection; ISO 27001:2022 A.7.4 identically requires monitoring of physical premises against unauthorised physical access.
VDA ISA 3.1.4 requires controls for working in secure areas (clean desk, supervised access); ISO 27001:2022 A.7.6 identically requires controls for working in secure areas.
VDA ISA 3.1.5 requires a clean-desk policy and screen-locking procedures; ISO 27001:2022 A.7.7 identically requires clear desk and clear screen policies.
VDA ISA 3.2.1 visitor management (registration, escort, badge) is a sub-aspect of ISO 27001:2022 A.7.2 (physical entry controls) which covers all forms of entry control including visitor procedures.
VDA ISA 3.2.2 protecting against physical/environmental threats (fire, flood, power) maps to ISO 27001:2022 A.7.5 (protecting against physical and environmental threats).
VDA ISA 3.2.3 requires protection of supporting utilities (power, cooling, UPS); ISO 27001:2022 A.7.11 identically requires protection of supporting utilities.
VDA ISA 3.2.4 requires protection of cabling and network infrastructure; ISO 27001:2022 A.7.12 identically requires cabling security against interception or damage.
VDA ISA 3.3.1 requires secure siting and protection of IT equipment; ISO 27001:2022 A.7.8 identically requires equipment siting and protection to reduce environmental risks.
VDA ISA 3.3.2 requires security of assets used off-premises (laptops, mobile devices); ISO 27001:2022 A.7.9 identically requires security of assets off-premises.
VDA ISA 3.3.3 requires secure handling and disposal of storage media; ISO 27001:2022 A.7.10 identically requires storage media to be managed through their lifecycle.
VDA ISA 3.3.4 requires secure disposal or re-use of equipment (data wiping/destruction); ISO 27001:2022 A.7.14 identically requires secure disposal or re-use of equipment.
VDA ISA 3.3.5 requires maintenance of equipment to ensure availability and integrity; ISO 27001:2022 A.7.13 identically requires equipment maintenance to ensure availability and integrity.
VDA ISA 4.1.1 requires an access control policy based on need-to-know/least-privilege; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.15 identically requires access control policies based on business and IS requirements.
VDA ISA 4.1.1 access rights provisioning and review maps to ISO 27001:2022 A.5.18 which requires access rights to be provisioned, modified and removed according to the policy.
VDA ISA 4.1.2 requires a formal identity management process (unique IDs, lifecycle management); ISO 27001:2022 A.5.16 identically requires identity management for the full identity lifecycle.
VDA ISA 4.1.3 requires management and restriction of privileged access rights; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.2 identically requires privileged access rights to be restricted and managed.
VDA ISA 4.1.3 privileged utility program control maps to ISO 27001:2022 A.8.18 (use of privileged utility programs) requiring their use to be restricted.
VDA ISA 4.2.1 requires password policies including complexity, length, and change intervals; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.17 identically requires management of authentication information.
VDA ISA 4.2.2 requires multi-factor authentication for remote and privileged access; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.5 identically requires secure authentication measures including MFA where appropriate.

VDA ISA 4.2.3 requires restricting access to information and system functions to authorised users; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.3 identically requires information access restriction according to the access control policy.
VDA ISA 4.2.4 restricting access to source code is a specific sub-requirement of ISO 27001:2022 A.8.4 (access to source code) which covers source code, development tools, and software libraries.

VDA ISA 4.3.1 requires controls and policies for remote working/home office; ISO 27001:2022 A.6.7 identically requires protection of information accessed, processed or stored at remote working locations.
VDA ISA 4.3.2 requires endpoint security for user devices (laptops, mobiles, removable media); ISO 27001:2022 A.8.1 identically requires user endpoint devices to be protected.
VDA ISA 5.1.1 requires network security controls to protect connected systems; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.20 identically requires network security controls to protect information in networks.
VDA ISA 5.1.2 requires network segregation separating sensitive from general-purpose segments; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.22 identically requires segregation of networks based on trust levels and function.
VDA ISA 5.1.3 requires security requirements for network services including contracts; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.21 identically requires security of network services including features and SLAs.
VDA ISA 5.1.4 requires web filtering to protect against malicious web content; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.23 identically requires web filtering to restrict access to potentially harmful websites.
VDA ISA 5.2.1 requires change management for IT systems including testing and approval; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.32 identically requires change management procedures for information processing facilities.
VDA ISA 5.2.2 requires capacity monitoring and planning for IT systems; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.6 identically requires monitoring and management of capacity to ensure required performance.

VDA ISA 5.2.3 requires malware protection controls including anti-virus and endpoint detection; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.7 identically requires protection against malware to be implemented and supported by user awareness.
VDA ISA 5.2.4 requires vulnerability management and timely patching; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.8 identically requires technical vulnerability management including timely identification and remediation.
VDA ISA 5.2.5 requires logging of security-relevant events on systems and applications; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.15 identically requires production, storage and protection of logs.
VDA ISA 5.2.5 log monitoring requirement maps to ISO 27001:2022 A.8.16 (monitoring activities) which requires networks, systems and applications to be monitored for anomalous behaviour.
VDA ISA 5.2.6 requires synchronisation of clocks across IT systems; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.17 identically requires clock synchronisation of all relevant information processing systems.
VDA ISA 5.2.7 requires documented operating procedures for IT operations; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.37 identically requires documented operating procedures for information processing facilities.
VDA ISA 5.2.8 (new in v6) IT service continuity planning maps to ISO 27001:2022 A.5.30 (ICT readiness for business continuity) requiring ICT continuity planning and testing.
VDA ISA 5.2.8 IT service continuity includes redundancy requirements mapping to ISO 27001:2022 A.8.14 (redundancy of information processing facilities).
VDA ISA 5.2.9 (new in v6) backup and recovery requirements map to ISO 27001:2022 A.8.13 identically requiring information backup and tested restoration procedures.
VDA ISA 5.3.1 requires IS requirements to be considered during the software development lifecycle; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.25 identically requires a secure development lifecycle policy.
VDA ISA 5.3.2 requires application security requirements to be defined and implemented; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.26 identically requires application security requirements to be specified.
VDA ISA 5.3.3 requires secure systems architecture and engineering principles; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.27 identically requires secure system architecture and engineering principles.
VDA ISA 5.3.4 requires secure coding practices and standards; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.28 identically requires secure coding principles to be applied to software development.
VDA ISA 5.3.5 requires security testing of applications before acceptance into production; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.29 identically requires security testing in development and acceptance.
VDA ISA 5.3.6 requires separation of development, test and production environments; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.31 identically requires separation of development, test and production environments.

VDA ISA 5.3.7 requires security of outsourced development including contractual requirements; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.30 identically requires outsourced development activities to be managed and supervised.
VDA ISA 5.3.8 requires protection of test data; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.33 identically requires test information to be selected, protected and managed appropriately.
VDA ISA 5.4.1 requires use of encryption to protect sensitive data at rest and in transit; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.24 identically requires the use of cryptography including key management.
VDA ISA 5.4.1 encrypted communication requirement partially overlaps with A.8.5 (secure authentication) for TLS/mutual-auth scenarios, but A.8.5 covers authentication controls more broadly.
VDA ISA 5.4.2 requires key management procedures for cryptographic keys; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.24 explicitly includes use of cryptography and management of cryptographic keys.
VDA ISA 5.5.1 requires data leakage prevention controls to protect confidential information; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.12 identically requires data leakage prevention measures to be applied.
VDA ISA 5.5.2 requires secure deletion of data that is no longer required; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.10 identically requires deletion of information when no longer required.

VDA ISA 5.5.3 data masking in non-production environments is a specific use-case subset of ISO 27001:2022 A.8.11 (data masking) which covers masking in accordance with classification policy more broadly.
VDA ISA 6.1.1 requires IS requirements to be established before entering supplier relationships; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.19 identically requires IS policies for supplier relationships to be established.
VDA ISA 6.1.2 requires supplier contracts to include IS requirements and audit rights; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.20 identically requires IS requirements to be addressed within supplier agreements.
VDA ISA 6.1.3 requires managing IS risks across the ICT supply chain; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.21 identically requires management of IS in the ICT supply chain.
VDA ISA 6.1.4 requires monitoring and periodic review of supplier security performance; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.22 identically requires monitoring, review and change management of supplier services.
VDA ISA 6.1.5 requires security requirements for cloud services and providers; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.23 identically requires IS for use of cloud services to be established and managed.

VDA ISA 7.1.1 requires identification and documentation of relevant legal and regulatory IS requirements; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.31 identically requires identification of legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements.
VDA ISA 7.1.2 requires compliance with intellectual property rights for software and content; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.32 identically requires intellectual property rights compliance.
VDA ISA 7.1.3 requires protection and retention of records according to legal requirements; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.33 identically requires protection of records from loss, destruction and falsification.
VDA ISA 7.1.4 requires privacy and protection of personal data in line with GDPR; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.34 identically requires privacy and protection of PII in accordance with applicable requirements.
VDA ISA 7.1.5 requires regular IS compliance checks against policies and standards; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.36 identically requires compliance with IS policies, rules and standards to be reviewed.
VDA ISA 7.2.1 requires protecting IS infrastructure during audit and testing activities; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.34 identically requires protection of information systems during audit testing.
VDA ISA 1.2.7 requires collection and analysis of threat intelligence to inform IS decisions; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.7 (new) identically requires threat intelligence to be collected and analysed.
VDA ISA 1.4.5 requires integration of IS into project management and change processes; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.8 identically requires IS to be integrated into project management.

VDA ISA 5.2.4 vulnerability management is broader than ISO 27001:2022 A.8.8 for TISAX AL3 contexts, requiring OT/ICS vulnerability tracking and automotive-specific patch timelines not covered in the ISO baseline.

VDA ISA 3.1.2 for AL3 (prototype protection level) includes stricter zone segregation, mantrap/airlock requirements, and biometric controls that exceed the general physical entry controls in ISO 27001:2022 A.7.2.

VDA ISA module 9 (Data Protection) 9.1.1 requires a processing activities register per GDPR Art. 30; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.34 references PII protection requirements but does not mandate the specific GDPR processing record structure.

VDA ISA Data Protection 9.1.1 (GDPR Art. 30 processing activities record) is broader than ISO 27001:2022 A.5.34 which only generally requires privacy and PII protection without specifying GDPR article-level documentation obligations.

VDA ISA Data Protection 9.2.1 (Technical and Organisational Measures / TOMs per GDPR Art. 32) exceeds ISO 27001:2022 A.5.34 which references PII protection but does not spell out the GDPR Art. 32 adequacy assessment and documentation.

VDA ISA Data Protection 9.3.1 (data processor agreements per GDPR Art. 28) is more specific than ISO 27001:2022 A.5.19 supplier relationship security, adding mandatory GDPR Art. 28 contract clauses not required by ISO.

VDA ISA Data Protection 9.4.1 data deletion/retention per GDPR data minimisation principle is a subset of ISO 27001:2022 A.8.10 (information deletion) which covers deletion more broadly including non-personal data.

VDA ISA Data Protection 9.5.1 data subject rights handling (GDPR Art. 15-22) is a specific subset of ISO 27001:2022 A.5.34 covering PII, but ISO does not enumerate individual DSAR procedures as TISAX data protection module does.
VDA ISA 1.3.5 requires asset ownership to be assigned for all information assets; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.9 requires an inventory including ownership of information and associated assets.
VDA ISA 5.1.5 requires network access control to enforce connection policies; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.20 requires networks to be protected and controlled to safeguard information in systems.
VDA ISA 5.2.10 email security controls (anti-spam, anti-phishing) map to ISO 27001:2022 A.8.7 (protection against malware) which includes controls against malicious email content.
VDA ISA 5.4.3 requires use of approved encryption algorithms and standards; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.24 requires use of cryptography rules including approved algorithms and key lengths.
VDA ISA 4.1.4 requires periodic review and recertification of access rights; ISO 27001:2022 A.5.18 explicitly requires access rights to be reviewed at regular intervals and upon role changes.
